opp.name: ITT_Spend_May'26
opp.next_step: 3/12/26 RH Spoke with Ronit to receive the elements needed to provide a paid pilot. Provide these details to Jochen once received.
opp.ais_why:
* ITT is a spend-analytics land-and-expand: a planned one-year analytics pilot off the back of the SPX FLOW acquisition, targeting tail-spend across two large, disparate-ERP supply bases ("disparate ERPs/taxonomies following the SPX FLOW acquisition").
* The opportunity is a spend-analytics pilot driven by post-acquisition complexity: "Limited visibility into spend... Manual purchasing activities, large supplier base, and disparate ERPs/taxonomies following the SPX FLOW acquisition." No AI requirement is articulated.
* Strategy is a one-year spend analytics pilot (ML iAnalyze/Merlin in scope) as a land-and-expand into S2P - analytics value, not AI excitement, is the driver; early (Qualified, 10%).

items.area: All MEDDPICC elements (Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, Competition) are genuinely unknown across every source
items.quote: Zero contact roles, no readable MEDDPICC Salesforce fields, no Avoma calls discovered

items.area: No stakeholder map: zero contact roles, Ronit and Jochen mentioned but not confirmed
items.quote: Zero OpportunityContactRole records; Ronit and Jochen referenced in Next_Step but no title, email, or role

items.area: No buyer engagement signal in 114 days
items.quote: Last touch 12 March 2026 per Next_Step; no Salesforce LastActivityDate, no Avoma calls, no tasks/events

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists anywhere in the evidence. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) were readable. No Avoma calls discovered. The only signal is the Next_Step reference to a 'paid pilot', but no pain, ROI target, savings goal, or operational impact is documented. Without a quantified value case, even a successful pilot has no commercial anchor. The move is to run a discovery call that surfaces the specific pain (cost leakage, cycle time, error rate, compliance risk) and quantifies the impact in dollars or hours saved.

sources: Next_Step 12 March 2026 references Ronit and Jochen
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Zero contact roles. Ronit is mentioned in the 12 March Next_Step as the source of pilot requirements, and Jochen is named as the recipient of those details, but neither is confirmed as a stakeholder (no contact role, no Avoma call, no email/title). No evidence of advocacy (introductions, internal docs shared, competitive blocking, agenda-driving). The 114-day silence since 12 March suggests Ronit either deprioritized this or lost internal sponsorship. The move is to re-engage Ronit, confirm their current role and interest, and assess whether they can champion this deal or whether a new internal advocate is needed.

competition.narrative: No competitor identified in any source. Competitors__c field unknown. No free-text fields readable. No Avoma calls. No Task or Next_Step references to other vendors. This could mean sole-sourced (ITT is only evaluating Zycus) or it could mean the competitive landscape is invisible to us. Given the 114-day silence, it is possible ITT moved forward with an incumbent or a competitor without telling us. The move is to ask directly on re-engagement whether they are evaluating other solutions and what their current stack looks like.

sources: Products field: ML iAnalyze;Merlin (inferred procurement/spend analytics need)
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain articulated. No Salesforce pain fields were readable. No Avoma calls. The only inference is that ITT is exploring ML iAnalyze and Merlin (per Products field), which implies a procurement or spend analytics need, but the specific pain owner, the cost of the current state, and the urgency driver are all unknown. Without confirmed pain, a pilot is an academic exercise. The move is to run a discovery call that names the pain owner, the current-state cost, and the consequence of inaction.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting or procurement mechanics documented. No Salesforce fields for paper process were readable. No Avoma calls. No indication of MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec requirements, or procurement channel (direct vs partner). The pilot framing suggests this may bypass formal procurement initially, but the path from pilot to $120k contract is undefined. The move is to confirm whether ITT procurement or legal will review the pilot agreement and what the full contracting process looks like.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified in any source. Zero contact roles on the opportunity. No Salesforce fields for EB (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c) were readable. No Avoma calls to reveal who controls budget or pricing conversations. A $120k SaaS+AI spend requires C-level or VP-level budget authority; the absence of any senior stakeholder engagement after 54 days in Qualified is a red flag that this may be an unqualified exploration rather than a funded initiative. The move is to map the budget owner and confirm funding before any pilot scoping.

sources: Next_Step 12 March 2026 references handoff to Jochen
decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. No Salesforce fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) were readable. Zero contact roles means we cannot read the approval chain from stakeholder composition. No Avoma call sequence to reveal who joins when or who escalates. The 12 March Next_Step mentions passing pilot details to Jochen, implying a handoff, but Jochen's role (technical lead, procurement, decision maker) is unknown. The move is to map the approval chain and confirm whether this is a single-approver or committee decision.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria documented. No Salesforce fields for criteria (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) were readable. No Avoma calls to capture use-case reviews, test cases, or RFP rounds. The pilot reference in Next_Step implies an evaluation intent, but without knowing what ITT will measure (accuracy, ease of integration, AI capability, ROI proof) we cannot shape the pilot to win. The move is to run a scoping call that extracts the specific success criteria and decision weights for the pilot and the full purchase.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Products field includes Merlin, indicating AI scope is part of the deal. No AIS score, status, or why fields populated. The Merlin inclusion suggests ITT has AI appetite, but without discovery calls or a quantified AI use case (negotiation agent, contract intelligence, supplier insights) we cannot confirm strength or under-positioning.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 4 is carried by momentum 50 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 4 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (4), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

confidence_signals.summary: Zero confidence signals. No buyer engagement in 114 days. No Salesforce LastActivityDate. No Avoma calls. Zero contact roles. No MEDDPICC coverage. No quantified business case. Deal is COLD per engagement pulse.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 May 2026, qualified same day, close date 20 Nov 2026 (138 days out). Time in Qualified stage: 54 days. No forward slip visible (last modified 18 June). No submission-date series fields populated. Last documented buyer touch: 12 March 2026 (Next_Step); 114 days of silence as of today (5 July 2026). No Salesforce LastActivityDate on record. Required pace to close: impossible to compute without any engagement signal. Verdict forced to Off Track by COLD forcing condition (60+ days no buyer touch).
evidence: No verified buyer engagement in 114 days (last touch 12 March per Next_Step)
Zero contact roles (no EB, DM, champion, or influencer mapped)
No Avoma calls discovered via opp-id or account-id pull
Pipeline forecast with no stakeholder map, no engagement, no MEDDPICC coverage is indefensible
north_star_verdict.headline: Dead silent for 114 days. No buyer engagement since 12 March Next_Step. Deal may be lost, shelved, or awarded to a competitor without our knowledge.

flags: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 106 days since last touch (12 March 2026 Next_Step). No buyer reply to the pilot offer, no calls, no logged activity. The deal owner must re-engage this week with a direct, senior buyer contact (target: the Global VP Division Chief Supply Chain Officer or the purchasing lead with spend authority) to confirm intent or mark the opportunity Closed Lost. A 3.5-month-cold deal in Qualified stage with 147 days to close and zero multi-thread is not a real Pipeline opportunity.

MAP THE ECONOMIC BUYER AND MULTI-THREAD: Zero buyer-side contact roles and no EB identified. A $120k spend decision requires budget authority and cross-functional buy-in (procurement, IT, finance). Before any pilot, map who controls the spend, who approves, and who will sponsor the business case. Add confirmed buyer stakeholders as contact roles on the opportunity.

BUILD A QUANTIFIED BUSINESS CASE: No pain or ROI documented. A pilot without a clear savings target, cycle-time improvement, or cost-reduction goal is a science experiment that will not convert. Co-author a value model with the buyer anchored to a specific operational or financial metric ITT cares about (e.g. indirect-spend visibility, supplier consolidation savings, contract compliance rate).
DARK: No buyer engagement in 114 days. Deal may be lost or shelved without our knowledge.
Zero contact roles: No EB, no DM, no champion, no influencers mapped. Single-thread risk is total.
No quantified business case: No pain, no ROI, no savings target. Pilot has no commercial foundation.
No MEDDPICC coverage: Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, and Competition all unmapped.
Forecast indefensible: Pipeline forecast on a 114-day dark deal with zero stakeholders is optimistic. Should be Omitted.
COLD: 114 days no buyer touch (last 12 March). Deal may be lost or shelved.
Zero contact roles: no EB, no DM, no champion, no exec sponsor. $120k spend with no senior stakeholder engagement.
No MEDDPICC coverage: no pain, no metrics, no criteria, no process, no champion, no competition mapped.
Single-thread: Ronit and Jochen referenced but unconfirmed (no contact role, no call). High ghost risk.
No quantified business case: pilot intent but no ROI, savings, or pain quantified.
Indefensible forecast: Pipeline with zero engagement, zero stakeholders, zero MEDDPICC.
Stage risk: 54 days in Qualified with no progression signal. Should be in discovery/demo if active.
best_practice_check.summary: Multiple critical best-practice violations. Dead silent for 114 days (forcing condition: COLD). Zero contact roles (no EB, DM, champion, influencer). No MEDDPICC coverage. No quantified business case. Single-thread to unconfirmed contacts (Ronit, Jochen). Pipeline forecast is indefensible.

The Merlin inclusion suggests ITT has AI appetite, but without discovery calls or a quantified AI use case (negotiation agent, contract intelligence, supplier insights) we cannot confirm strength or under-positioning.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No evaluation criteria or buyer requirements documented, so fit cannot be assessed. The only signal is the product scope (ML iAnalyze and Merlin per Products field), which implies ITT wants spend analytics with AI capability. But without knowing their success criteria, integration requirements, or decision weights, we cannot map our strengths. The pilot framing suggests they want proof before commitment, but what they will measure in the pilot is undefined.

* The opportunity is a spend-analytics pilot driven by post-acquisition complexity: "Limited visibility into spend... Manual purchasing activities, large supplier base, and disparate ERPs/taxonomies following the SPX FLOW acquisition." No AI requirement is articulated.

deltas.subject: Forecast indefensible: Pipeline forecast on a 114-day dark deal with zero stakeholders is optimistic. Should be Omitted.
Dead silent for 3.5 months. No buyer engagement since 12 March 2026 Next_Step. The deal may already be lost, shelved, or awarded to a competitor without our knowledge.
No Economic Buyer, no champion, no executive sponsor, zero contact roles. A $120k spend with zero senior stakeholder engagement is unlikely to close.
Single-thread (Ronit, Jochen referenced but not confirmed as stakeholders; zero contact roles). No multi-thread, no power-map, high ghost risk.

RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 106 days since last touch (12 March 2026 Next_Step). No buyer reply to the pilot offer, no calls, no logged activity. The deal owner must re-engage this week with a direct, senior buyer contact (target: the Global VP Division Chief Supply Chain Officer or the purchasing lead with spend authority) to confirm intent or mark the opportunity Closed Lost. A 3.5-month-cold deal in Qualified stage with 147 days to close and zero multi-thread is not a real Pipeline opportunity.

MAP THE ECONOMIC BUYER AND MULTI-THREAD: Zero buyer-side contact roles and no EB identified. A $120k spend decision requires budget authority and cross-functional buy-in (procurement, IT, finance). Before any pilot, map who controls the spend, who approves, and who will sponsor the business case. Add confirmed buyer stakeholders as contact roles on the opportunity.

BUILD A QUANTIFIED BUSINESS CASE: No pain or ROI documented. A pilot without a clear savings target, cycle-time improvement, or cost-reduction goal is a science experiment that will not convert. Co-author a value model with the buyer anchored to a specific operational or financial metric ITT cares about (e.g. indirect-spend visibility, supplier consolidation savings, contract compliance rate).
